Qual a melhor abordagem para configurar Security List para load balancer no contexto da LogiTrack Logistics projeto security-hardening? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 64OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#128]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para security - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci vault secret create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "security-data-platform-127" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci vault secret list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"secret-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci <service> update \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci vault secret get \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci vault secret schedule-secret-deletion \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.A1.Flex`
- OCPUs: `64`
- Storage: `100 GB`

Melhores praticas para otimizar regras de seguranca no contexto AgriTech Innovations/hybrid-cloud-integration (devops, me-jeddah-1) [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 32OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#110]

**Security Audit — Security (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Best practices para security - LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de security
- Escolha a regiao `ap-sydney-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `analytics` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation`
  - `environment: analytics`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

**Configuracao de referencia:**
- Shape: `VM.GPU.A10.1`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `1000 GB`
- Region: `ap-sydney-1`

**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Como implementar problemas de observability com resolver para a AgriTech Innovations no projeto hybrid-cloud-integration (devops, me-jeddah-1)? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#90]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Observability (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id devops \
  --display-name "high-cpu-devops" \
  --metric-compartment-id devops \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs

A SmartCity IoT precisa resolver problemas de observability para o projeto backup-strategy. Como fazer no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 1OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#73]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para observability - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "observability-staging-072" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"is-enabled"=='true'].{name:"display-name",metric:"metric-compartment-id"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm update \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm get \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm delete \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `1`
- Storage: `50 GB`

Documentacao: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs

**Integration Pattern — DevOps (InsureTech Plus - identity-federation)**

**CI/CD Pipeline:**
```
Git Repo → OCI DevOps Build → OCIR → OKE / Compute
                ↓
        OCI DevOps Deploy
                ↓
        OCI Functions / Compute Instance Group
                ↓
        OCI Monitoring (health check)
```

**Infrastructure as Code:**
- Terraform via OCI Resource Manager
- State management em Object Storage
- Plans automaticos em MRs
- Apply via pipeline aprovado

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Melhores praticas para configurar devops no contexto FoodChain Trace/database-consolidation (data-platform, ca-toronto-1) [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#138]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para devops - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci devops project create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "devops-data-platform-137" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci devops project list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci devops project update \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci devops project get \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci devops project delete \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `2`
- Storage: `2000 GB`

**Disaster Recovery — State (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

A EnergyGrid Monitor precisa resolver problemas de state para o projeto serverless-transformation. Como fazer no compartment security? [context: shape=BM.GPU4.8, 1OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#17]

**Security Audit — State (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Documentacao: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs

- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)

- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

Implementacao via OCI CLI para state - EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci os bucket create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='security'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "state-security-086" \
  --region ap-tokyo-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci os bucket list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",tier:"storage-tier"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci os bucket update \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "security", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci os bucket get \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci os bucket delete \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `security`
- Region: `ap-tokyo-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`

Como planejar e executar configurar log disaster recovery para FinServe Digital no projeto disaster-recovery-setup (eu-frankfurt-1)? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 8OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=192.168.0.0/16, ticket#84]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Logging (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/overview.htm

Qual a melhor abordagem para configurar service logs no contexto da AgriTech Innovations projeto hybrid-cloud-integration? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#30]

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para logging - AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci logging log-group get --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `devops`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

**Integration Pattern — Observability (SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to manage objects in compartment production
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to use secret-family in compartment production
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

A LegalDoc Manager precisa configurar log export Object Storage para o projeto network-segmentation. Como fazer no compartment analytics? [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#59]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para logging - LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci logging log-group create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='analytics'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "logging-analytics-058" \
  --region ap-sydney-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci logging log-group list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci <service> update \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "analytics", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci logging log-group get \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci logging log-group delete \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `analytics`
- Region: `ap-sydney-1`
- Shape: `VM.GPU.A10.1`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

A equipe da EnergyGrid Monitor quer configurar log compliance para serverless-transformation. Qual configuracao no compartment security? [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 16OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#77]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Logging (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id security \
  --display-name "high-cpu-security" \
  --metric-compartment-id security \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Monitoring & Alerting — Monitoring (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id development \
  --display-name "high-cpu-development" \
  --metric-compartment-id development \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

Como configurar alarm suppression com foco em configurar para AgriTech Innovations no projeto hybrid-cloud-integration? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 2OCPUs, 500GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#10]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para monitoring - AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='devops'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "monitoring-devops-009" \
  --region me-jeddah-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"is-enabled"=='true'].{name:"display-name",metric:"metric-compartment-id"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm update \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "devops", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm get \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm delete \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `devops`
- Region: `me-jeddah-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `2`
- Storage: `500 GB`

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

Passo a passo para criar dashboards OCI Monitoring da FoodChain Trace (projeto database-consolidation, ca-toronto-1, data-platform) [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#18]

Best practices para monitoring - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de monitoring
- Escolha a regiao `ca-toronto-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `data-platform` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation`
  - `environment: data-platform`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

**Configuracao de referencia:**
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `2`
- Storage: `2000 GB`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`

- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Quais etapas para configurar Monitoring automation da CloudNative Inc (projeto microservices-refactor, compartment staging)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#83]

**Integration Pattern — Observability (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'staging'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'staging'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group staging-functions to manage objects in compartment staging
Allow dynamic-group staging-functions to use secret-family in compartment staging
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'data-platform'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'data-platform'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group data-platform-functions to manage objects in compartment data-platform
Allow dynamic-group data-platform-functions to use secret-family in compartment data-platform
```

Melhores praticas para configurar Monitoring automation no contexto AutoDrive Systems/container-platform (networking, ap-mumbai-1) [context: shape=VM.Standard3.Flex, 64OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#96]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Monitoring (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id networking \
  --display-name "high-cpu-networking" \
  --metric-compartment-id networking \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Como configurar Monitoring reports com foco em configurar para LogiTrack Logistics no projeto security-hardening? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 8OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=192.168.0.0/16, ticket#108]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para monitoring - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "monitoring-data-platform-107" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"is-enabled"=='true'].{name:"display-name",metric:"metric-compartment-id"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm update \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm get \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm delete \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `8`
- Storage: `2000 GB`

Orientacoes para configurar alertas Stack Monitoring no cenario da FinServe Digital - projeto disaster-recovery-setup, eu-frankfurt-1, sandbox [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 64OCPUs, 500GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#64]

Best practices para stack-monitoring - FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de stack-monitoring
- Escolha a regiao `eu-frankfurt-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `sandbox` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup`
  - `environment: sandbox`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

**Configuracao de referencia:**
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `64`
- Storage: `500 GB`
- Region: `eu-frankfurt-1`

**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Descreva o processo de configurar Stack Monitoring agents para SmartCity IoT (backup-strategy) em staging/us-phoenix-1 [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 16OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#93]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para stack-monitoring - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "stack-monitoring-staging-092" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",type:"resource-type"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target update \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target get \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target delete \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `200 GB`

A EnergyGrid Monitor esta no projeto serverless-transformation e precisa configurar Stack Monitoring thresholds. Como proceder no security (ap-tokyo-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 1OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#137]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Stack Monitoring (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id security \
  --display-name "high-cpu-security" \
  --metric-compartment-id security \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para stack-monitoring - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci stack-monitoring target get --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `data-platform`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Como configurar Stack Monitoring credentials com foco em configurar para LogiTrack Logistics no projeto security-hardening? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 8OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=192.168.0.0/16, ticket#108]

**Integration Pattern — Observability (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'data-platform'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'data-platform'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group data-platform-functions to manage objects in compartment data-platform
Allow dynamic-group data-platform-functions to use secret-family in compartment data-platform
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

A equipe da MediaStream Pro quer criar custom spans APM para multi-region-expansion. Qual configuracao no compartment analytics? [context: shape=BM.Standard.E5, 1OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#49]

**Integration Pattern — Observability (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'analytics'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'analytics'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group analytics-functions to manage objects in compartment analytics
Allow dynamic-group analytics-functions to use secret-family in compartment analytics
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

```bash
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

```

A CloudNative Inc precisa instrumentar aplicacoes APM para o projeto microservices-refactor. Como fazer no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 4OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#3]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para apm - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci apm domain create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "apm-staging-002" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci apm domain list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci apm domain update \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci apm domain get \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci apm domain delete \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `200 GB`

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/apm/overview.htm

Quais sao os requisitos para visualizar service maps APM no ambiente da LegalDoc Manager (network-segmentation)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 4OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#19]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Apm (LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)

Melhores praticas para configurar APM dashboards no contexto GameForge Studios/zero-trust-implementation (sandbox, eu-frankfurt-1) [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#54]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Apm (GameForge Studios - zero-trust-implementation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Metodologia de benchmark:**

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume

```

Quais etapas para diagnosticar cross-compartment connectivity da BioResearch Labs (projeto api-gateway-deployment, compartment shared-services)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 48OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#55]

**Security Audit — Connectivity (BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Orientacoes para diagnosticar Load Balancer connectivity no cenario da LogiTrack Logistics - projeto security-hardening, ca-toronto-1, data-platform [context: shape=BM.GPU4.8, 64OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#8]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para connectivity - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci network vcn create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "connectivity-data-platform-007" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci network vcn list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",cidr:"cidr-block"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci network vcn update \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci network vcn get \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci network vcn delete \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `BM.GPU4.8`
- OCPUs: `64`
- Storage: `100 GB`

Como diagnosticar instance network problems para SmartCity IoT (backup-strategy) no compartment staging (us-phoenix-1)? [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 1OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#113]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para connectivity - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci network vcn create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "connectivity-staging-112" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci network vcn list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",cidr:"cidr-block"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci network vcn update \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci network vcn get \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci network vcn delete \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.GPU.A10.1`
- OCPUs: `1`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

**Performance Tuning — Performance (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

You are an OCI troubleshooting specialist with expertise in performance. Provide diagnostic guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and network performance.

Como planejar e executar diagnosticar memory usage problems para HealthTech Systems no projeto cost-optimization (ap-mumbai-1)? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#126]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Performance (HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id networking \
  --display-name "high-cpu-networking" \
  --metric-compartment-id networking \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

```bash
oci compute instance launch \

  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation"}}' \
```

```bash
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

- Region: `ap-sydney-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para performance - LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci compute instance get --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `analytics`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

**Security Audit — Authentication (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado

- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

Descreva o processo de diagnosticar identity provider failures para CloudNative Inc (microservices-refactor) em staging/us-phoenix-1 [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 48OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#23]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para authentication - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci iam compartment create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "authentication-staging-022" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci iam compartment list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci iam user update \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci iam user get \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci iam user delete \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.GPU.A10.1`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para authentication - TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci iam user get --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `production`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Quais etapas para diagnosticar service account problems da CloudNative Inc (projeto microservices-refactor, compartment staging)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#83]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para authentication - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci iam compartment create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "authentication-staging-082" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci iam compartment list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci iam user update \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci iam user get \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci iam user delete \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.A1.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

```

Best practices para database - AutoDrive Systems - container-platform:

- Defina claramente os requisitos de database
- Escolha a regiao `ap-mumbai-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `networking` para isolamento logico

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: AutoDrive Systems - container-platform`
  - `environment: networking`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

A equipe da SecureBank Corp quer diagnosticar database scaling problems para ci-cd-pipeline. Qual configuracao no compartment production? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 4OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#91]

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para database - SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci db autonomous-database get --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `production`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

A EduPlatform Global precisa diagnosticar storage capacity problems para o projeto performance-tuning. Como fazer no compartment security? [context: shape=VM.Standard3.Flex, 48OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#87]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para database - EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='security'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "database-security-086" \
  --region ap-tokyo-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",db:"db-name"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database update \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "security", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database get \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database delete \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `security`
- Region: `ap-tokyo-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`

**Performance Tuning — Database (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

**Disaster Recovery — Database (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

Best practices para database - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de database
- Escolha a regiao `ca-toronto-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `data-platform` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation`
  - `environment: data-platform`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

**Configuracao de referencia:**
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `32`
- Storage: `500 GB`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`

**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

A equipe da CloudNative Inc quer diagnosticar console connection problems para microservices-refactor. Qual configuracao no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 48OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#63]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para compute - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "compute-staging-062" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`

Best practices para compute - LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de compute
- Escolha a regiao `ap-sydney-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `analytics` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation`
  - `environment: analytics`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

- Shape: `VM.Standard.E3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `50 GB`
- Region: `ap-sydney-1`

- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Best practices para storage - GameForge Studios - zero-trust-implementation:

- Implemente tagging consistente:

  - `environment: sandbox`
  - `managed-by: terraform`

- Implemente backup automatizado

- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `32`
- Storage: `2000 GB`
- Region: `eu-frankfurt-1`

A CloudNative Inc precisa diagnosticar download performance problems para o projeto microservices-refactor. Como fazer no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 4OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#3]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para storage - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "storage-staging-002" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci bv volume list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",size:"size-in-gbs"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci bv volume get \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci bv volume delete \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `200 GB`

A SecureBank Corp esta no projeto ci-cd-pipeline e precisa diagnosticar cluster upgrade failures. Como proceder no production (sa-saopaulo-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#11]

**Integration Pattern — Oke (SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline)**

**Servicos OCI que integram com oke:**
1. **OCI Vault**: Encryption keys e secrets management
2. **OCI Monitoring**: Metricas e alertas
3. **OCI Logging**: Logs estruturados
4. **OCI IAM**: Politicas de acesso
5. **OCI Notifications**: Alertas e eventos

**Padrao comum:**
```
Recurso OCI → Events → Service Connector Hub → Destination
                                    ↓
                        ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                        ↓           ↓           ↓
                  Functions   Notifications  Streaming
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to manage objects in compartment production
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to use secret-family in compartment production
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Quais sao os requisitos para diagnosticar cluster autoscaler problems no ambiente da EnergyGrid Monitor (serverless-transformation)? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 16OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#117]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para oke - EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci ce cluster create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='security'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "oke-security-116" \
  --region ap-tokyo-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci ce cluster list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",k8s:"kubernetes-version"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci ce cluster update \
  --cluster-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "security", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci ce cluster get \
  --cluster-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci ce cluster delete \
  --cluster-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `security`
- Region: `ap-tokyo-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `200 GB`
